syncedFromCode: BuildPlan.status: added SUPERSEDED value (set by Update Build Plan on the prior version)
Unit.shipmentId: added nullable FK populated by Units Picked And Packed
Change Material ETA: added rejection-branch GWT for equal-or-earlier ETA

stack.frontend: Vanilla JS + Tailwind (CDN) — served statically from /web

persistenceDecisions: Owned-entity collections are stored in separate tables with FK + ON DELETE CASCADE to the aggregate root (BomItem→Project, BuildDemand→Build, Unit→Shipment). Embedding as JSON would block the read-model queries that filter on child attributes (e.g. List Builds At Risk needs PO ETAs).
Optimistic locking via a `version` integer column on every aggregate root; child entities update through the root, so they don't need their own version column.

Cross-bounded-context references (e.g. Build.siteId → Compass.ProductionSite, PurchaseOrder.projectId → PRIM.Project) are stored as plain string FKs without Prisma @relation — honoring the BC boundary.

Unit gets an optional `shipmentId` FK so the picked-and-packed transition can group units into a shipment in one transactional write. The unit's primary owner remains Build (`buildId`), matching the spec.
BuildPlan.versionNo (the model field) was renamed in storage to avoid clashing with the optimistic-lock column also named `version`.
Append-only `EventLog` table records every emitted event (and skipped no-op markers) so the simulator UI can drive step-by-step playback and the audience can see which row just changed.

openItems.issue: Build Plan archival semantics on Update Build Plan
openItems.current: Creating v(N+1) moves builds from v(N) to v(N+1) and marks v(N) as SUPERSEDED. This keeps the build's FK pointing to exactly one current plan version.
openItems.alternative: Keep builds attached to v(N) and treat v(N+1) as informational only. Spec §2 says 'previous version archived' without specifying ownership transfer; current choice was made to keep downstream Lock/Release readable.

openItems.issue: Material Shortage Identified and Material Kit Completed are derived events
openItems.current: Wired as subscribers on the in-process event bus. They fire automatically after MaterialETAChanged and MaterialReceivedAtSite respectively.
openItems.alternative: Could be modeled as scheduled SQL queries that run on a tick; current choice is simpler and matches the spec's 'rules engine = two SQL queries that run after every event'.

openItems.current: Handlers read role from the x-role HTTP header (or AUTH_ROLE env var). Role→command mapping is enforced inside each handler via assertRole.
openItems.alternative: Replace src/auth.ts with JWT/OAuth before deploying. Handler code does not need to change.

openItems.issue: Validator's MISSING_COLLECTION_IDENTIFIER warning on Update/Lock Build Plan
openItems.current: Accepted as legitimate. Neither command mutates a specific Build; the validator flags them only because the BuildPlan aggregate declares a `builds` relationship.
openItems.alternative: If a future delta adds per-build identification to those commands (e.g. partial replan), the warning will need re-evaluation.
